title: Ghazala Siddique

infobox.value: |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Women's [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") | | | | Representing  [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") | | | | [South Asian Games](/wiki/South_Asian_Games "South Asian Games") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [2019 Kathmandu-Pokhara](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2019_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2019 South Asian Games") | Women's team | |
also spelt **Ghazala Saddique** (born 12 April 1994),[[2]](#cite_note-2) is a [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player from [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan").

At the 57th National Badminton Championship held in Lahore, Pakistan, Siddique was runners-up in the singles event, losing in the final (19-21, 13-21) to defending champion [Mahoor Shahzad](/wiki/Mahoor_Shahzad "Mahoor Shahzad").[[4]](#cite_note-4) In the women's doubles event final, she and her partner, [Zubaira Islam](/w/index.php?title=Zubaira_Islam&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zubaira Islam (page does not exist)") (SNGPL) were defeated by Mahoor Shahzad (Wapda) and [Palwasha Bashir](/wiki/Palwasha_Bashir "Palwasha Bashir") ([NBP](/wiki/National_Bank_of_Pakistan "National Bank of Pakistan")) 2-1 (11-21, 21-10, 3-21).[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) In the mixed doubles final, she and her partner, M Ali Larosh were beaten by [Zunain Javed](/w/index.php?title=Zunain_Javed&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zunain Javed (page does not exist)") and [Farzana Ali](/w/index.php?title=Farzana_Ali&action=edit&redlink=1 "Farzana Ali (page does not exist)") 2-0 (12-21, 20-22).[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

["Mahoor, Murad grab national badminton singles titles"](https://nation.com.pk/01-Feb-2020/mahoor-murad-grab-national-badminton-singles-titles). *The Nation*. 2020-02-01. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20200805055357/https://nation.com.pk/01-Feb-2020/mahoor-murad-grab-national-badminton-singles-titles) from the original on 2020-08-05. Retrieved 2020-11-16.

["Badminton"](https://www.13sagnepal.com/sport/badminton/). *South Asian Games Nepal 2019*. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20201026092658/https://www.13sagnepal.com/sport/badminton/) from the original on 2020-10-26. Retrieved 2020-11-16.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Pakistani badminton player

Ghazala Siddique

| Personal information | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1994-04-12) 12 April 1994 (age 31)  [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") |
| Sport | |
| Country | [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") |
| Sport | [Badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") |
|  | |
| Singles and mixed doubles | |
| Highest ranking | 505 (WS 16 August 2018) [[1]](#cite_note-bwfranking-1)   493 (XD 17 March 2020)[[1]](#cite_note-bwfranking-1) |
| Current ranking | 588 (WS 16 November 2020)   493 (XD 16 November 2020) |
| [BWF profile](https://bwfbadminton.com/player/81451) | |
|  | |
| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Women's [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") | | | | Representing  [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") | | | | [South Asian Games](/wiki/South_Asian_Games "South Asian Games") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [2019 Kathmandu-Pokhara](/wiki/Badminton_at_the_2019_South_Asian_Games "Badminton at the 2019 South Asian Games") | Women's team | | |

**Ghazala Siddique,** also spelt **Ghazala Saddique** (born 12 April 1994),[[2]](#cite_note-2) is a [badminton](/wiki/Badminton "Badminton") player from [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ghazala_Siddique&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

### National

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ghazala_Siddique&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: National")]

Siddique represents [WAPDA](/wiki/Water_%26_Power_Development_Authority "Water & Power Development Authority") in domestic competitions including National Championships[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) and National Games.

**2020**

At the 57th National Badminton Championship held in Lahore, Pakistan, Siddique was runners-up in the singles event, losing in the final (19-21, 13-21) to defending champion [Mahoor Shahzad](/wiki/Mahoor_Shahzad "Mahoor Shahzad").[[4]](#cite_note-4) In the women's doubles event final, she and her partner, [Zubaira Islam](/w/index.php?title=Zubaira_Islam&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zubaira Islam (page does not exist)") (SNGPL) were defeated by Mahoor Shahzad (Wapda) and [Palwasha Bashir](/wiki/Palwasha_Bashir "Palwasha Bashir") ([NBP](/wiki/National_Bank_of_Pakistan "National Bank of Pakistan")) 2-1 (11-21, 21-10, 3-21).[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3) In the mixed doubles final, she and her partner, M Ali Larosh were beaten by [Zunain Javed](/w/index.php?title=Zunain_Javed&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zunain Javed (page does not exist)") and [Farzana Ali](/w/index.php?title=Farzana_Ali&action=edit&redlink=1 "Farzana Ali (page does not exist)") 2-0 (12-21, 20-22).[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

### International

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ghazala_Siddique&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: International")]

In 2014, Siddiqui participated in singles and mixed doubles events at The Indian Club Centenary Li-Ning Bahrain International Challenge held in [Manama](/wiki/Manama "Manama"), [Bahrain](/wiki/Bahrain "Bahrain"). In the singles she lost in the round of 32 to [Indian](/wiki/India "India"), [Aparna Balan](/wiki/Aparna_Balan "Aparna Balan") (14-21, 5-21) while in the mixed doubles, pairing with [Muhammad Irfan Saeed Bhatti](/wiki/Muhammad_Irfan_Saeed_Bhatti "Muhammad Irfan Saeed Bhatti"), she lost in the round of 16 to the Indian pair of [Arun Vishnu](/wiki/Arun_Vishnu "Arun Vishnu") and Aparna Balan (11-21,7-21).[[5]](#cite_note-:1-5) In 2016, at the Yonex Sunrise Pakistan International Series she competed in singles, doubles and mixed doubles events. In singles, she lost to compatriot, [Sehra Akram](/wiki/Sehra_Akram "Sehra Akram") in the round of 16 (18-21, 9-21).[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6) In the mixed doubles she teamed up with [Anjum Bashir](/w/index.php?title=Anjum_Bashir&action=edit&redlink=1 "Anjum Bashir (page does not exist)"). They lost to compatriots, Muhammad Irfan Saeed Bhatti and Mehmona Ameer (15-21, 17-21) in the quarter-finals.[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6) In the women's doubles she teamed up with [Javeria Tahir](/w/index.php?title=Javeria_Tahir&action=edit&redlink=1 "Javeria Tahir (page does not exist)"). They lost in the semi-finals to compatriots, [Sidra Hamad](/wiki/Sidra_Hamad "Sidra Hamad") and [Khizra Rasheed](/wiki/Khizra_Rasheed "Khizra Rasheed") (18-21, 18-21).[[6]](#cite_note-:2-6) In 2017, she once again competed at the Yonex Sunrise Pakistan International Series event in [Islamabad](/wiki/Islamabad "Islamabad"), though only in singles and mixed doubles. In singles she lost to compatriot, Sehra Akram in the quarter-finals (21-23, 10-21).[[7]](#cite_note-:3-7) In the mixed doubles, teaming up with [Muhammad Attique](/w/index.php?title=Muhammad_Attique&action=edit&redlink=1 "Muhammad Attique (page does not exist)") she lost in the quarter-finals to the [Nepali](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal") pair of [Bishnu Katwal](/w/index.php?title=Bishnu_Katwal&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bishnu Katwal (page does not exist)") and [Sunayana Mukhiya](/w/index.php?title=Sunayana_Mukhiya&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sunayana Mukhiya (page does not exist)") (21-15, 21-23, 19-21).[[7]](#cite_note-:3-7) At the [Asian Games](/wiki/2018_Asian_Games "2018 Asian Games") held in [Jakarta](/wiki/Jakarta "Jakarta"), [Indonesia](/wiki/Indonesia "Indonesia") in 2018, she competed only in the women's double event where pairing with [Saima Waqas](/wiki/Saima_Manzoor "Saima Manzoor") she won her first match again the [Maldives](/wiki/Maldives "Maldives") pair of Neela Najeeb and Moosa Aminath Shahurunaz (22-20, 18-21, 21-15).[[8]](#cite_note-:4-8) She then lost in the round of 16 to the [Japanese](/wiki/Japan "Japan") pair of [Misaki Matsutomo](/wiki/Misaki_Matsutomo "Misaki Matsutomo") and [Ayaka Takahashi](/wiki/Ayaka_Takahashi "Ayaka Takahashi") (3-21, 3-21).[[8]](#cite_note-:4-8) Siddique was part of the six member women's team which competed at the [2019 South Asian Games](/wiki/2019_South_Asian_Games "2019 South Asian Games") held in [Kathmandu](/wiki/Kathmandu "Kathmandu"), [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Events participated in:

1. The Indian Club Centenary Li-Ning Bahrain International Challenge (2014)
2. Yonex- Sunrise Pakistan International Series (2016)
3. Yonex- Sunrise Pakistan International Series (2017)
4. Asian Games (2018)
5. South Asian Games (2019)

Registered but did not participate in:

1. Dong Feng Citroen Badminton Asia Championships, [Wuhan](/wiki/Wuhan "Wuhan"), [China](/wiki/China "China") (2015) (singles and mixed doubles)[[5]](#cite_note-:1-5)
